authorities.name: U.S. Geological Survey (Geographic Names Information System)

sources.title: Nevada Board of Wildlife Commissioners, CR 25-16, Fishing Seasons and Regulations (Jan. 1, 2026 – Dec. 31, 2027) — Douglas County, "Lake Tahoe and tributaries" listing
sources.quotedText: Lake Tahoe and tributaries. Open year around. Daily: 5 game fish. Possession: 10 game fish. Hours: 1 hour before sunrise to 2 hours after sunset.

sources.title: CR 25-16 — Douglas County CLOSED WATERS (Lincoln Creek not listed as closed; only Glenbrook Creek and the Cave Rock boat-launch area are)
sources.quotedText: Topaz Lake within the jetties of Topaz Marina. Lake Tahoe within the boat launch area, inside the jetty at the Cave Rock boat ramp, north end. Glenbrook Creek from Lake Tahoe to Highway 50.

sources.title: Apply & Buy — Nevada Fishing Licenses — NDOW
sources.quotedText: Anyone 12 years of age or older is required to have a fishing license in their possession while in the field.

sources.title: OpenStreetMap way 1382632090 — "Lincoln Creek", a mapped east-shore stream reaching Lake Tahoe near Cave Rock (downstream terminus ~39.0399, -119.9490, Douglas Co., NV)
USGS GNIS (The National Map Gazetteer) — Streams layer record for gaz_id 860343, "Lincoln Creek", Stream, Douglas Co., NV
sources.quotedText: {"gaz_id":860343,"gaz_name":"Lincoln Creek","gaz_featureclass":"Stream","fcode":46000,"state_alpha":"NV","county_name":"Douglas"} — geometry multipoint; first point (Web Mercator -13352763.7127, 4727310.7998) converts to lon -119.94992, lat 39.03936 (mouth, near Lake Tahoe/Cave Rock), ~108 m from the file's recorded coordinate (-119.94877, 39.03975), confirming it. Second point (Web Mercator -13346435.3433, 4727085.1150) converts to lon -119.89307, lat 39.03778, the headwater end further up the Carson Range.

parameters.note: Lincoln Creek is a short east-shore Lake Tahoe tributary in Douglas County, Nevada; its mapped course runs west down the Carson Range and enters the lake just south of Cave Rock (OpenStreetMap way 1382632090, downstream terminus ~39.0399, -119.9490), so it is a Lake Tahoe tributary. It is not named individually in CR 25-16, so the applicable Douglas County listing is 'Lake Tahoe and tributaries' — open year around. It is NOT in Douglas County's closed-waters list: the only Cave Rock closure is 'Lake Tahoe within the boat launch area, inside the jetty at the Cave Rock boat ramp, north end,' which is the boat-ramp basin and does not include Lincoln Creek. That 'Lake Tahoe and tributaries' row (rather than the county's general 'All waters' row) governs Lake Tahoe's tributary streams, as confirmed by the parallel Washoe County treatment where specific Lake Tahoe tributary creeks (Third, Incline, Wood) are individually closed 'from Lake Tahoe to the Mt. Rose Highway.'

regulations.citation: NV CR 25-16 (Douglas County — Lake Tahoe and tributaries)
regulations.humanSummary: Open year-round (Nevada / Douglas County 'Lake Tahoe and tributaries' listing; Lincoln Creek is a Lake Tahoe tributary and is not in the county's closed-waters list).
regulations.verbatimText: Lake Tahoe and tributaries. Open year around.

parameters.counting_rule: Douglas County 'Lake Tahoe and tributaries' limit: 5 game fish daily, 10 game fish in possession. 'Game fish' is the combined coldwater/warmwater game-fish count; no species sub-limit is listed for this row in CR 25-16.

regulations.citation: NV CR 25-16 (Douglas County — Lake Tahoe and tributaries)
regulations.humanSummary: 5 game fish per day, 10 in possession (Nevada Douglas County 'Lake Tahoe and tributaries' limit).
regulations.verbatimText: Lake Tahoe and tributaries. Daily: 5 game fish. Possession: 10 game fish.

parameters.note: The 'Lake Tahoe and tributaries' row carries restricted hours (1 hour before sunrise to 2 hours after sunset) — narrower than Douglas County's general 'All waters' rule of any hour of the day or night.

regulations.citation: NV CR 25-16 (Douglas County — Lake Tahoe and tributaries)
regulations.humanSummary: Fishing permitted from 1 hour before sunrise to 2 hours after sunset.
regulations.verbatimText: Hours: 1 hour before sunrise to 2 hours after sunset.

parameters.note: A Nevada fishing license is required for anglers 12 and older. Lincoln Creek is a Nevada tributary (Douglas Co.), not the open water of Lake Tahoe; the Lake Tahoe CA/NV reciprocal-license arrangement is written for fishing anywhere ON the lake, so a Nevada license (not a California license alone) should be carried on the creek.

regulations.humanSummary: A Nevada fishing license is required for anglers age 12 and older; none required under 12.
regulations.verbatimText: Anyone 12 years of age or older is required to have a fishing license in their possession while in the field.
